2603.22707.title: Detecting Non-Membership in LLM Training Data via Rank Correlations
2603.22707.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) are trained on increasingly vast and opaque text corpora, determining which data contributed to training has become essential for copyright enforcement, compliance auditing, and user trust. While prior work focuses on detecting whether a dataset was used in training (membership inference), the complementary problem -- verifying that a dataset was not used -- has received little attention. We address this gap by introducing PRISM, a test that detects dataset-level non-membership using only grey-box access to model logits. Our key insight is that two models that have not seen a dataset exhibit higher rank correlation in their normalized token log probabilities than when one model has been trained on that data. Using this observation, we construct a correlation-based test that detects non-membership. Empirically, PRISM reliably rules out membership in training data across all datasets tested while avoiding false positives, thus offering a framework for verifying that specific datasets were excluded from LLM training.
2603.22707.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）在越來越龐大且不透明的文本語料庫上進行訓練，確定哪些數據對訓練有所貢獻已成為版權執法、合規審計和用戶信任的重要課題。<lb/>雖然先前的研究專注於檢測數據集是否被用於訓練（成員推斷），但互補問題——驗證數據集未被使用——卻鮮有關注。<lb/>我們通過引入PRISM來解決這一空白，這是一種僅使用灰箱訪問模型邏輯來檢測數據集級別非成員資格的測試。<lb/>我們的關鍵見解是，兩個未見過某數據集的模型在其標準化的標記對數概率中表現出更高的排名相關性，而當其中一個模型已在該數據上進行訓練時則會降低。<lb/>利用這一觀察，我們構建了一個基於相關性的測試來檢測非成員資格。<lb/>實證結果顯示，PRISM可靠地排除了所有測試數據集中訓練數據的成員資格，同時避免了假陽性，從而提供了一個框架來驗證特定數據集是否被排除在LLM訓練之外。
2603.22707.author: Pranav Shetty et.al.
2603.22707.authors: Pranav Shetty, Mirazul Haque, Zhiqiang Ma, Xiaomo Liu
